result:
Build succeeds. Here's what was built for **Round 69**:

---

## Symmetry Group Detection Overlay (Overlay #36)

**Key: Ctrl-G** | **New analytical domain: discrete group theory**

Classifies every cell's 5×5 neighborhood into its discrete symmetry group by testing all rotational and reflective invariances of the local patch. Nine symmetry groups detected:

| Group | Order | What it means |
|-------|-------|---------------|
| C1 | 1 | No symmetry (asymmetric) |
| C2 | 2 | 180° rotational symmetry only |
| D1h/D1v/D1d/D1a | 2 | Single reflection axis |
| D2 | 4 | Half-turn + 2 reflection axes |
| C4 | 4 | Pure 4-fold rotation (no reflections) |
| D4 | 8 | Full dihedral — all symmetries of the square |

**Color scheme**: dark charcoal (C1) → blue-cyan spectrum (C2/D1) → green/gold (D2/C4) → brilliant white-gold (D4)

**Sidebar panel**: mean symmetry order with classification, dominant group, breaking/forming event rates, 9-group histogram, color gradient legend, sparkline of Ō(t)

**Symmetry-breaking detection**: tracks frame-over-frame transitions from higher to lower symmetry order — the hallmark of phase transitions in physics. Complements Fisher Information by revealing *what structural order exists* on each side of phase boundaries.

+480 lines, clean compile, full split-screen support.
